title: ISO/IEC 42001 — AI management system
name: ISO/IEC 42001
summary: International standard for establishing, implementing, maintaining and continually improving an AI management system within organizations.
qa_relevance: Map QA gates, roles and evidence to AI MS controls for audit-ready testing governance.

title: ISO/IEC 23894 — AI risk management guidance
name: ISO/IEC 23894
summary: Guidance on how organizations can integrate risk management into AI-related activities and functions throughout the AI lifecycle.
qa_relevance: Drive risk-based test prioritization, data quality checks and human oversight scenarios from risk registers.

title: ISO/IEC 5338 — AI system life cycle processes
name: ISO/IEC 5338
summary: Defines processes for AI system development, operation and maintenance across the full AI lifecycle.
qa_relevance: Place verification and validation gates at lifecycle transitions: data prep, training, deployment and monitoring.

title: ISO/IEC 25059 — Quality model for AI systems
name: ISO/IEC 25059
summary: Extends SQuaRE with AI-specific quality characteristics: functional correctness, robustness, explainability and data quality.
qa_relevance: Define measurable AI acceptance criteria and test cases per quality characteristic before release.

title: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 — Software testing
name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119
summary: Series of standards for test processes, documentation, techniques and keyword-driven testing.
qa_relevance: Structure test policies, plans, cases and reports so AI-assisted and manual testing stay auditable.

title: ISO/IEC 33001 — Process assessment
name: ISO/IEC 33001
summary: Concepts and terminology for process assessment and capability determination of organizational processes.
qa_relevance: Assess testing process maturity before scaling AI codegen or automation investments.

title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
name: NIST AI RMF
summary: Voluntary framework to manage AI risks through Govern, Map, Measure and Manage functions.
qa_relevance: Align QA workflows with RMF functions: govern test policy, map risks, measure behavior, manage incidents.

title: NIST Secure Software Development Framework
name: NIST SSDF
summary: Core practices for secure software development: prepare, protect, produce, respond and govern.
qa_relevance: Apply SSDF to AI-assisted development: threat modeling, review, SAST, dependency checks and provenance.

title: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
name: EU AI Act
summary: EU regulation establishing harmonized rules for AI systems, including conformity requirements for high-risk AI.
qa_relevance: Plan conformity tests, technical documentation, logging, human oversight and post-market monitoring evidence.

title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
name: OWASP LLM Top 10
summary: Top security risks for applications using large language models, including prompt injection and insecure output.
qa_relevance: Test LLM-integrated apps and codegen pipelines against adversarial prompts and unsafe output handling.

title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
name: OWASP ASVS
summary: Structured security requirements for designing, building and testing modern web applications.
qa_relevance: Use ASVS levels as a structured security test backlog complementary to functional QA.

title: OWASP Web Security Testing Guide
name: OWASP WSTG
summary: Practical guide for web application security testing organized by vulnerability categories.
qa_relevance: Rotate WSTG checks aligned to threat model as security smoke tests each sprint.

title: IEEE 1012 — Software verification and validation
name: IEEE 1012
summary: Standard for software verification and validation planning, including independence and anomaly resolution.
qa_relevance: Plan V&V activities with traceability and independence suited to AI-augmented components.

title: WCAG 2.2 — Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
name: WCAG 2.2
summary: W3C recommendation for making web content accessible, including new success criteria for focus and target size.
qa_relevance: Integrate WCAG 2.2 criteria into design, development and release accessibility gates.

title: OECD AI Principles
name: OECD AI Principles
summary: International recommendations on trustworthy AI: inclusive growth, human rights, transparency, robustness and accountability.
qa_relevance: Anchor internal QA AI policy on OECD principles for executive and cross-border alignment.
